description: Refactor verification, DER parsing, extension handling, and public result models before the first stable release.

tasks.title: Add shared result and error primitives
tasks.description:
Create public and internal result primitives in `src/result.ts` and `src/core/result.ts`. Done when:
- `Result<T, E>` and base error shapes exist
- at least one domain module can import them without cycles
- the design clearly supports indexed verify failures and details-rich revocation errors

tasks.title: Harden and centralize the DER spine
tasks.description:
Tighten low-level DER handling and make top-level parsers consume stricter shared helpers. Done when:
- length encoding/decoding rejects overflow and non-minimal encodings
- top-level parse entrypoints reject trailing DER data
- bounded nesting checks exist for hostile deeply nested input
- malformed regression tests cover the new failure cases

tasks.title: Re-home public types to domain modules
tasks.description:
Move shared public types out of `src/validation.ts` into domain-owned modules. Done when:
- policy types live in `src/policy.ts`
- name-constraint types live in `src/name-constraints.ts`
- identity and revocation types live with their owning domains
- `src/validation.ts` is removed or reduced to a transient shim with an explicit removal step

tasks.title: Extract the policy engine from verify
tasks.description:
Move RFC 9618 policy state and graph logic into `src/verify/policy-engine.ts`. Done when:
- `src/verify.ts` delegates policy evaluation to one internal engine
- policy state, transitions, and outcome derivation no longer live inline in `verify.ts`
- existing policy and verify suites still cover the extracted behavior

tasks.title: Extract the name-constraints engine from verify
tasks.description:
Move name-constraint accumulation, matching, and directoryName subtree logic into `src/verify/name-constraints-engine.ts`. Done when:
- `src/verify.ts` no longer owns per-name-form matcher logic
- directoryName subtree comparison is isolated from verify orchestration
- existing verify coverage plus focused internal tests protect the extracted engine

tasks.title: Add the cert and CSR extension registry
tasks.description:
Create `src/extensions/registry.ts` for built-in certificate and CSR extensions. Done when:
- built-in extension definitions own OID, encode, decode, and parse-assignment metadata
- registry scope is explicitly limited to certificate and CSR extensions
- verifier critical-extension support remains separate from registry support

tasks.title: Rewire parse and builder paths to use the registry
tasks.description:
Replace the built-in extension switch in `src/parse.ts` and the duplicated built-in builder lists in `src/extensions.ts`. Done when:
- cert/CSR parse dispatch uses registry definitions
- cert/CSR builder paths use the same registry definitions
- round-trip tests prove no semantic loss for built-in extensions

tasks.title: Redesign verify and identity public APIs
tasks.description:
Adopt nested verify-domain inputs and shared result shapes across verify and identity surfaces. Done when:
- verify inputs use nested `policy` and `nameConstraints` objects
- verify and identity public failures are reported through the shared result model
- `verify.ts` adapts identity failures at the composition seam instead of duplicating matcher concerns

tasks.title: Redesign CRL, OCSP, and revocation result surfaces
tasks.description:
Normalize revocation-facing APIs onto the shared result model without changing core parse-throw semantics. Done when:
- CRL, OCSP, and revocation public results use `Result<T, E>`
- status-bearing outcomes move into `value` payloads instead of mixed top-level unions
- malformed-input behavior remains a parse-boundary concern, not a revocation-result concern

tasks.title: Normalize PFX and PKCS7 result surfaces
tasks.description:
Bring PFX and PKCS7 public results onto the shared result model while preserving their domain-specific parse semantics. Done when:
- PFX and PKCS7 public results use shared primitives
- malformed/password/content-type distinctions stay explicit
- tests prove the container APIs still surface the right failure category

tasks.title: Finalize exports, docs, and regression coverage
tasks.description:
Finish the clean break by updating barrels, build/export config, docs, and the full validation matrix. Done when:
- public exports point at the new domain-owned types and facades
- spec/docs describe the new API boundaries and result model
- `bun typecheck`, `bun test`, and `bun lint` pass after the refactor set
